actual_meaning: please take the required action
note: Indian-English officialese; literal works but sounds odd. Hindi/Tamil dubs usually rephrase to a natural 'please handle it' rather than translate word-for-word.

actual_meaning: reply / get back to me
note: Indian-English misuse of 'revert' to mean 'reply'. A literal dub would mean 'undo'. Hindi/Tamil should render as 'jawab dena' / 'reply pannu' to avoid the wrong sense.

actual_meaning: please reply to me
note: Doubly redundant Indian-English. Dubs drop the 'back' and translate to a plain 'please reply'.

actual_meaning: move an event/meeting to an earlier time
note: Indian-English coinage; no single global English word. Hindi/Tamil have natural phrasings ('pehle kar dena' / 'munnadiya maathu') so the concept dubs fine even though the word is uniquely Indian.

actual_meaning: an update / updating something
note: Non-standard Indian-English noun. Concept is trivially translatable; only the odd word form is Indian. Dubs just say 'update'.

actual_meaning: to inform / notify formally
note: Indian-English formal usage meaning 'inform'. Literal sense ('intimate/close') is misleading. Dubs use plain 'inform' / 'soochit karna' / 'theriyappaduthu'.

actual_meaning: out of town / away from one's city
note: Indian-English idiom. Literal mentions a railway station; meaning is 'travelling/away'. Hindi/Tamil say 'shaher se bahar' / 'oorla illa'.

actual_meaning: please (formal request marker)
note: Heavy Indian-English politeness marker. Translates cleanly as 'kripaya' / 'tayavu seydhu'; the only flavour lost is the slightly over-formal officialese tone.

actual_meaning: graduating from a school/college
note: Indian-English where 'passing out' = graduating, not fainting. A literal dub flips the meaning entirely. Hindi/Tamil must use 'pass hona / graduate hona' to avoid suggesting someone collapsed.

actual_meaning: message me (on chat/Slack)
note: Workplace English. Indian audiences know 'ping'; often kept as a loanword. Dubs can say 'mujhe message karna' / 'enaku message pannu'.

actual_meaning: end of day (deadline by close of business)
note: Corporate acronym. Usually spoken as letters and kept as-is in Indian offices; dubs may expand to 'din ke ant tak' / 'naal mudivuku munnadi' for clarity.

actual_meaning: work from home
note: Pandemic-era acronym, fully naturalised in Indian English. Kept as a loanword in speech; dubs can expand to 'ghar se kaam' / 'veetla irundhu velai'.

actual_meaning: out of office (auto-reply / unavailable)
note: Corporate acronym. Less universally known than WFH in India; dubs often expand to 'office mein nahi hoon' / 'office la illa'.

actual_meaning: have a quick alignment meeting
note: Corporate jargon. Kept as loanword among professionals; for general audiences dubs say 'baat kar lete hain' / 'pesi mudikkalaam'.

literal: go around in a circle and return
actual_meaning: return to a topic / follow up later
note: Corporate cliche. Literal is meaningless; dubs use 'baad mein baat karte hain' / 'apparam pesalaam'.

actual_meaning: briefly check in / reconnect
note: Baseball-derived idiom opaque to Indian audiences. No literal sense survives; dubs rephrase to 'thoda update le lete hain' / 'konjam pesalaam'.

actual_meaning: a person's available time/capacity to take on work
note: Tech metaphor for personal capacity. Often kept in English in Indian offices; dubs render the meaning as 'time nahi hai' / 'time illa' or 'capacity'.

actual_meaning: too busy / little spare capacity
note: Office jargon. Dubs convey as 'abhi time nahi hai' / 'ippo time illa'; the network metaphor is dropped.

actual_meaning: relentless hard work / a money-making venture
note: Creator/startup buzzword. Indians often keep 'hustle' in English; dubs can use 'mehnat' / 'kashtam' but lose the aspirational grind connotation.

actual_meaning: a secondary income gig beside one's main job
note: Gig-economy term. Usually kept in English; dubs explain as 'extra kamai ka kaam' / 'pakka velai' if needed.

actual_meaning: sustained relentless effort
note: Hustle-culture term ('on the grind'). Dubs use 'mehnat / din raat kaam' but the gritty self-motivation vibe is hard to carry; often kept in English.

actual_meaning: a startup self-funded without outside investors
note: VC jargon from an opaque English idiom. Indian founders keep 'bootstrapped' in English; dubs paraphrase as 'apne paise se shuru kiya' / 'sontha panathula thodanga'.

actual_meaning: months of cash a startup has before it runs out
note: Startup-finance metaphor. Literal (airstrip) misleads; the term is kept in English. Dubs must paraphrase 'paisa kitne mahine chalega' / 'panam evlo naal varum'.

actual_meaning: how fast a startup spends its cash
note: VC jargon. Kept in English by Indian founders; dubs paraphrase as 'kitna paisa har mahine kharch ho raha hai'.

actual_meaning: a hands-on, intense way a startup founder runs things (2024 viral term)
note: 2024 Paul Graham coinage, very niche. No equivalent; kept in English in Indian tech videos. Dubs would have to explain it, not translate.

actual_meaning: the ownership/equity breakdown of a company
note: Startup-finance term. Always kept in English; no Hindi/Tamil equivalent in common use. Dubs leave it as a loanword.

actual_meaning: non-binding document outlining investment terms
note: VC document name; kept in English. Dubs leave as loanword or briefly gloss 'investment ki shartein'.

actual_meaning: a content collaboration between creators
note: Creator-economy clip. Indians use 'collab' in English; dubs can say 'saath mein video' but usually keep the loanword.

actual_meaning: to shoot an Instagram Reel
note: Hinglish; 'reel' is a fixed loanword + Hindi verb. Already half-Hindi, so it dubs naturally. Tamil: 'reel pannu'. No translation of 'reel' needed.

actual_meaning: the content blew up / got huge reach
note: Hinglish; 'viral' is a universal loanword. Dubs keep 'viral' and swap the verb: Tamil 'viral aagiduchu'. No real translation difficulty.

actual_meaning: currently popular / on the trending list
note: Universal platform loanword. Indian audiences use 'trending' as-is; dubs rarely translate it.

actual_meaning: please subscribe to the channel
note: Hinglish creator catchphrase; 'subscribe' is a fixed YouTube loanword. Dubs keep 'subscribe', adapt the verb. Tamil: 'subscribe pannunga'.

actual_meaning: the standard creator call-to-action
note: Three platform loanwords in a row. Indian creators say them in English even within Hindi/Tamil videos; no translation expected.

actual_meaning: please tap the like button enthusiastically
note: YouTube catchphrase. 'Like' stays English; 'smash' is hyperbole. Dubs say 'like button dabao' / 'like pannunga', losing the aggressive 'smash' flavour.

actual_meaning: an original / the real veteran or pioneer
note: Hip-hop acronym used admiringly. Indians keep 'OG' in English; no clean equivalent. Dubs gloss as 'asli / purana wala' but lose the cred.

actual_meaning: greatest of all time
note: Acronym G.O.A.T. The literal animal is a false friend — risky to dub literally. Kept in English; dubs may gloss 'sabse mahaan' / 'eppovume best'.

actual_meaning: a win / something good
note: Single-letter internet slang. Untranslatable as a letter; dubs replace with 'win' / 'jeet' / 'good' or keep 'W' on-screen text. Spoken Hindi/Tamil would just say 'badhiya'.

actual_meaning: a loss / a fail
note: Single-letter slang ('take the L'). Untranslatable phonetically; dubs render as 'haar' / 'loss' or keep on-screen. The letter itself carries no meaning in Hindi/Tamil.

actual_meaning: a reply outperforming the original post (social-media dunk)
note: Twitter/X-specific. Literal 'ratio' is a false friend and meaningless here. No Hindi/Tamil equivalent; kept in English and usually explained.

actual_meaning: no lie / I'm being serious
note: AAVE slang where 'cap' = lie. Literal ('hat') is a false friend. Dubs convey as 'sach mein' / 'unmaiya' but the phrase itself stays English among youth.

actual_meaning: mediocre / unimpressive
note: Gen-Z dismissal. Literal 'middle' loses the dismissive sneer. Dubs use 'theek-thaak / bekaar' / 'avlo illa' but the cool clipped tone is lost; often kept in English.

actual_meaning: admirably authentic / unafraid to hold an opinion
note: Internet slang (from Lil B). Meaning is opaque and ironic; literal 'based on' is a false friend. No Hindi/Tamil equivalent; kept in English.

actual_meaning: acting like the protagonist of your own life; confident and central
note: Film-derived vibe phrase. Concept is explainable but no idiom matches; dubs paraphrase 'hero wali feel' / 'hero maadhiri'. The compact slang punch is hard to keep.

actual_meaning: playfully delusional / having unrealistic hopes
note: 2024-25 viral clip of 'delusional'. The cutesy reduplication is the whole joke and is untranslatable; dubs keep 'delulu' or weakly gloss 'sapne dekhna'.

actual_meaning: coolness / charisma you gain or lose by your actions
note: Gen-Z reuse of 'aura'. Hindi/Tamil have 'aura' as a known word but not the gamified slang sense; dubs keep 'aura' and rely on context.

actual_meaning: imaginary coolness score that goes up or down with your behaviour
note: 2025 meme construct. No equivalent; the gamified humour is the point. Dubs keep 'aura points' in English with context.

actual_meaning: charm / flirting ability
note: 2023-25 viral term. The clipped form is untranslatable; dubs keep 'rizz' or paraphrase 'set karne ki kala' / 'charm', losing the slang freshness.

actual_meaning: a dramatic improvement in looks/life
note: Common Gen-Z term. Concept dubs well as 'transformation' / 'maaruthal'; Indians often keep 'glow up' in English.

actual_meaning: doomed / finished / in deep trouble
note: Gen-Z slang ('we're cooked'). Literal food sense is a false friend; dubs convey as 'gaye / khatam' / 'mudinju pochu'. The slang stays English among youth.

actual_meaning: fully focused / in deep concentration
note: 2025 focus-culture term. Literal ('imprisoned') misleads. Dubs paraphrase 'pura focus' / 'full concentration'; the slang is often kept in English.

actual_meaning: somewhat / secretly / subtly
note: Hedging adverb. No neat Hindi/Tamil match; dubs use 'thoda sa' / 'konjam' but lose the understated cool. Often kept in English.

actual_meaning: openly / very much / no secret about it
note: Opposite of lowkey. Dubs paraphrase 'bilkul / khulke' / 'fullaa'; the paired slang symmetry is hard to keep.

actual_meaning: it gives off the vibe of / it resembles
note: AAVE construction with no object. Grammatically untranslatable into Hindi/Tamil; dubs must rephrase entirely to 'aisa lag raha hai' / 'maadhiri irukku'.

actual_meaning: performed excellently / nailed it
note: AAVE slang ('she ate'). Literal 'ate food' is a total false friend — dangerous to dub literally. Dubs render as 'kamaal kar diya' / 'அசத்திட்டாங்க', dropping the slang.

actual_meaning: to do something impressively / look amazing
note: Gen-Z praise. Literal 'kill' misleads. Dubs use 'cha gaye' / 'அசத்திட்ட'; Indians also keep 'slay' in English.

actual_meaning: someone who acts robotic / without independent thought
note: Gaming acronym turned insult. Kept in English; literal expansion needs gaming knowledge. Dubs gloss 'bina dimaag ke / robot jaisa' but lose the gamer flavour.

actual_meaning: a lone, self-reliant 'alpha-adjacent' male (often ironic)
note: Manosphere/meme term. No equivalent; kept in English. Literal 'Greek letter' is meaningless. Dubs leave 'sigma' as-is with context.

actual_meaning: unsolicited preachy advice / lecturing
note: Hindi word used ironically across India. Literal 'knowledge' loses the sarcastic 'preachy lecture' sense. Tamil keeps 'gyaan' as borrowed slang or uses 'class edukkurathu'.

actual_meaning: stop lecturing / spare me the unsolicited advice
note: Hindi idiom. The sarcasm of 'gyaan' is key. Tamil renders 'class edukatha' / 'gyaan kudukatha'; literal 'don't give knowledge' misses the bite.

actual_meaning: a shady fix / dodgy workaround in a deal
note: Hindi slang for a shady arrangement. Literal 'sag/loose' is meaningless. Tamil has no neat match; dubs paraphrase 'gadbad / scene' or keep 'jhol'.

actual_meaning: to arrange/fix things / make something happen via maneuvering
note: Hinglish; 'scene' is a fixed slang loanword. Dubs keep 'scene' and adapt: Tamil 'scene set pannu'. Concept of arranging a deal carries fine.

actual_meaning: one's web of useful professional contacts
note: Business loanword. Indians keep 'network' / 'networking' in English; dubs may gloss 'sampark / contacts' / 'thodarbu' but the verb 'networking' stays English.

actual_meaning: influential people one knows who can help
note: Indian-English for useful acquaintances. Translates as 'jaan-pehchaan' / 'pidippu' but 'contacts' is usually kept in English.

actual_meaning: connections/influence used to get things done (Indian-English)
note: Distinctly Indian-English noun ('he has approach'). The connections sense doesn't exist in global English and 'approach' literal is a false friend. Dubs render as 'pahunch' / 'pidippu'.

actual_meaning: getting something via personal favour or influence
note: Hindi/Urdu word, widely understood. Carries a slightly murky favouritism nuance. Tamil uses 'recommendation / pidippu'; literal 'recommendation' undersells the backdoor connotation.

actual_meaning: a clever frugal hack/workaround
note: Iconic Indian concept; no English equivalent. Often kept as 'jugaad' even in English. Tamil borrows it or uses 'thantram'; literal translation loses the resourceful spirit.

actual_meaning: a fix/arrangement is in place (often shady or romantic)
note: Hinglish slang where 'setting' = a fixed arrangement or a relationship. Literal 'setting' is a false friend. Tamil 'setting irukku' borrows it; dubs keep the loanword.

actual_meaning: a startup valued at over $1 billion
note: VC term. Literal animal is a false friend. Always kept in English in Indian tech; dubs leave 'unicorn' as-is, sometimes glossing 'billion dollar startup'.

actual_meaning: a startup changing its core business direction
note: Startup jargon. Literal 'turn' undersells the strategic shift. Kept in English; dubs paraphrase 'business badalna' / 'maathuthal' losing the term.

actual_meaning: minimum viable product (a basic first version)
note: Startup acronym; also collides with 'most valuable player'. Kept in English; no Hindi/Tamil equivalent, dubs spell it out or gloss.

actual_meaning: the point where a product strongly satisfies real demand
note: Startup phrase, often said as 'PMF'. Conceptually explainable but kept in English in Indian tech talks; dubs would lecture, not translate.

actual_meaning: venture capital / venture capitalist (startup investor)
note: Finance acronym. Kept in English; Hindi/Tamil have no native term, dubs use 'investor' / 'muthaleedu' as a loose gloss.

actual_meaning: the startup raised investment
note: Hinglish; 'funding' is a fixed loanword. Dubs keep 'funding' and swap the verb. Tamil 'funding vandhuduchu'. Easy.

actual_meaning: the assessed monetary worth of a startup
note: Finance term. Indians keep 'valuation' in English; dubs can gloss 'company ki keemat' / 'company mathippu' but the term usually stays.

actual_meaning: employee stock ownership plan (equity given to staff)
note: HR/finance acronym. Always kept in English; no Hindi/Tamil equivalent. Dubs leave 'ESOPs' as-is, optionally glossing 'company ke shares'.

actual_meaning: an unsolicited outreach email to a stranger
note: Startup outreach term. Literal 'cold' is metaphorical. Dubs keep 'cold email' or gloss 'anjaan ko bheja email'; usually loanword.

actual_meaning: send me a direct/private message on social media
note: Universal platform acronym. Indians say 'DM' in English; dubs keep it. Tamil 'DM pannu'. No translation needed.

actual_meaning: likes/comments/shares a post attracts
note: Creator-metrics loanword. Kept in English; dubs can gloss 'logon ka response' but creators use 'engagement' as-is.

actual_meaning: secretly limiting a creator's reach without telling them
note: Platform slang. Literal opaque; kept in English. Dubs paraphrase 'reach chupke se kam karna' / 'reach kammi pannrathu'.

actual_meaning: misleading sensational title to lure clicks
note: Creator term. Concept dubs as 'jhansa wala title' but Indians keep 'clickbait' in English; widely understood loanword.

actual_meaning: the recommendation algorithm that decides reach
note: Creator shorthand ('the algo gods'). 'Algorithm' itself is a loanword; the clipped 'algo' is kept in English. Dubs leave as-is.

actual_meaning: to earn revenue from content/a product
note: Creator/business term. Indians keep 'monetize' in English; dubs gloss 'paisa kamaana' / 'panam sambadikkurathu' but lose the platform-specific sense.

actual_meaning: to hoard info/access and not share it
note: Internet slang. Literal 'keep a gate' is opaque. Dubs paraphrase 'chupakar rakhna' / 'kaata pakkuradhu'; the slang stays English.

actual_meaning: to show off / brag
note: Hip-hop slang, widely used in India. Dubs use 'dikhaava / sheikhi' / 'kaattratu' but Indians often keep 'flex' in English.

actual_meaning: the mood/feel of a person or place
note: Ubiquitous Gen-Z loanword. Dubs gloss 'mahaul / feel' / 'feel' but 'vibe' is kept in English across Indian languages.

actual_meaning: assessing someone's mood/energy
note: Internet phrase. 'Vibe' is a loanword; the playful idiom is hard to translate neatly. Dubs keep 'vibe check' or paraphrase 'mood dekhna'.

actual_meaning: embarrassing / second-hand awkward
note: Gen-Z loanword fully naturalised in India ('kitna cringe hai'). Dubs keep 'cringe'; Hindi 'sharminda karne wala' is clunky.

actual_meaning: suspicious / shady
note: Among Us-popularised clip. Indians keep 'sus' in English; dubs gloss 'shak wala / gadbad' / 'doubt' but lose the clipped meme flavour.

actual_meaning: social-media influence/fame
note: Creator slang ('clout chasing'). No neat Hindi/Tamil word; kept in English. Dubs paraphrase 'naam-shohrat ke liye'.

actual_meaning: considered the greatest
note: Verb form of GOAT. Literal 'made a goat' is a comical false friend. No equivalent; kept in English. Dubs paraphrase 'sabse bada legend'.

actual_meaning: lazy, unkempt, zero-effort self-indulgence
note: 2022 viral term. No equivalent; the goblin imagery is the joke. Kept in English; dubs would have to explain it.

actual_meaning: go outside / log off and reconnect with reality
note: Internet insult to chronically-online people. Literal works visually but the idiom won't land. Tamil/Hindi have no match; dubs paraphrase 'bahar nikal' / 'veliya po'.

actual_meaning: content designed to make people angry for engagement
note: 2025 creator term. Compound is opaque; dubs paraphrase 'gussa dilane wala content'. Kept in English by creators.

actual_meaning: mindless low-quality content / its dulling effect
note: 2024 word-of-the-year. Literal dubs oddly; dubs gloss 'dimaag kharab karne wala content' / 'moolaiya kettuvidura content'. Often kept in English.

actual_meaning: to kill/die (censorship-dodging euphemism)
note: Algorithm-evasion coinage. The whole point is the English euphemism for moderation; Hindi/Tamil have no censored equivalent. Dubs must rephrase or keep English.

actual_meaning: an enemy / rival
note: Hip-hop slang. Dubs use 'dushman' / 'edhiri' but the clipped street tone is lost; youth keep 'opp' in English.

actual_meaning: to excessively praise/flatter someone
note: 2024-25 slang. Literal 'glaze' (icing) is a false friend. No Hindi/Tamil match; dubs paraphrase 'zyada tareef karna' / 'over praise pannrathu'.

actual_meaning: to talk too much / ramble
note: 2024 slang ('yapping', 'yapper'). Dubs use 'bakbak karna' / 'pita pidikkurathu' but Indians keep 'yap' in English for the meme tone.

actual_meaning: a cute affectionate nickname for someone
note: Internet pet-name. No translation; the cutesy sound is the point. Kept in English; dubs may swap to 'jaan / cutie' but lose the meme.

actual_meaning: an aesthetic/genre suffix (e.g. cottagecore, corporate-core)
note: Suffix slang. Untranslatable as a productive suffix; literal 'core' is a false friend. Kept in English in Indian content.

actual_meaning: a personal phase one is embracing ('my X era')
note: Taylor-Swift-popularised slang ('villain era'). Literal 'era' undersells the playful self-branding. Dubs paraphrase 'mera daur' / 'en kaalam'; often kept in English.

actual_meaning: caught red-handed with clear proof
note: Internet idiom referencing video quality. Literal '4K' needs explaining. Dubs paraphrase 'rang-e-haath pakda gaya' / 'kaiyodu pidipattu', losing the meme.

actual_meaning: really good (usually food)
note: AAVE slang. Untranslatable form; literal sense opaque to Indians. Dubs gloss 'mast / lajawaab' / 'sema' but the word stays English.

literal: it hits in a different way
actual_meaning: feels uniquely better / specially good
note: Gen-Z phrase. Literal 'hits' is metaphorical. Dubs paraphrase 'alag hi maza hai' / 'vera level' but the compact idiom is hard to keep.

actual_meaning: a founder being uniquely suited to their startup's problem
note: VC jargon, very niche. Kept in English in Indian tech circles; dubs would have to explain the concept rather than translate it.

actual_meaning: a startup operating secretly before public launch
note: Startup term. Literal 'stealth' is metaphorical military borrowing. Kept in English; dubs gloss 'chupke se kaam kar rahe hain'.

actual_meaning: I have attached a file for you
note: Indian/British email officialese. Literal 'find' sounds odd; dubs rephrase to a natural 'file bhej rahi hoon' / 'file anuppuren'.

actual_meaning: exactly identical
note: Indian-English emphatic reduplication. Concept ('bilkul same' / 'same maadhiri') dubs easily; the doubled English construction is the only Indian flavour.
